name: Seated Cable Row
resistance_profile: Even tension with a strong stretch at full reach; stable enough to train hard.
rom_notes: Allow the shoulder blades to protract and arms to extend fully forward, then row to the torso.
cues: Row to the lower ribs, drive the elbows back
execution_steps: Sit at the cable station with feet on the platform and knees slightly bent.
Grab the handle and sit tall with arms extended, feeling a stretch at the front.
Row the handle to your lower ribs, driving the elbows back.
Squeeze the shoulder blades, then return under control to a full stretch with minimal torso swing.
good_when: You want smooth constant tension and a strong stretch to train the back near failure.
You want a stable row that's easy to progressively overload without lower-back strain.
bad_when: You want to train the hinge and lower-back stabilization (use a barbell row).
Only free weights are available, with no cable stack.
